drills.name: Wall Serve Progression
drills.description: Players serve into a wall from 15 feet, then 20, then 30, checking toss and contact each rep before backing up. Ten serves per distance.
drills.coachingPoints: Toss in front of your hitting shoulder, firm hand through the middle of the ball.

drills.name: Zone Target Serving
drills.description: Cones mark zones 1, 5, and 6 on the far court. Coach calls a zone; every server has three attempts to land it there. Track team makes.
drills.coachingPoints: Pick your zone before the toss and hold your follow-through toward it.

drills.name: Pressure Serving Ladder
drills.description: Team must make five straight serves in to move up a rung; a miss drops them one rung. First side to the top rung wins.
drills.coachingPoints: Same routine every serve — breathe, toss, contact — especially when the streak is on the line.

drills.name: Partner Platform Pepper
drills.description: Pairs pass back and forth on a flat forearm platform from ten feet, working to 25 consecutive controlled passes without the ball touching the floor.
drills.coachingPoints: Flat platform away from your body, angle it to the target — legs pass the ball, not arms.

drills.name: Butterfly Serve Receive
drills.description: Continuous loop: serve from one end line, receiver passes to a target at the net, then everyone follows their ball — server to receiver, receiver to target, target to serving line.
drills.coachingPoints: Beat the ball to your spot and be stopped before contact.

drills.name: Shuffle-and-Pass Windows
drills.description: Passer shuffles between two cones six feet apart; a tosser alternates balls to each cone and the passer must arrive, stop, and pass to target.
drills.coachingPoints: Shuffle, stop, pass — never swing your platform while you're still moving.

drills.name: Free Ball Call and Convert
drills.description: Coach sends easy free balls over the net; the receiving three must call 'mine,' pass to the setter spot, and convert set-and-hit over the net.
drills.coachingPoints: Call it loud and early — a silent free ball is how easy points get dropped.

drills.name: Setter Triangle
drills.description: Tosser at position 6, setter at the net, target at position 4. Setter squares to target and delivers hittable sets from tosses of varying quality. Ten reps, rotate.
drills.coachingPoints: Square your shoulders to zone 4 and finish with hands extended to the target.

drills.name: Catch-and-Release Setting
drills.description: Pairs set back and forth; beginners briefly catch in the shape above the forehead and release, then progress to clean live contact. Work to 20 in a row.
drills.coachingPoints: Big hands shaped to the ball above your forehead — push through with legs and wrists.

drills.name: Chase Set Recovery
drills.description: Coach tosses a bad pass away from the net; the setter sprints, gets under it, and delivers a high outside set a hitter can attack. Rotate setters each rep.
drills.coachingPoints: Sprint first, set second — get your feet there so the set looks normal.

drills.name: Approach Footwork Lines
drills.description: Players rep the three-step spike approach (left-right-left for righties) along the net with a full arm swing and block-touch jump, no ball. Ten approaches each.
drills.coachingPoints: Slow to fast — big final two steps, both arms swing back then up.

drills.name: Toss-and-Swing Mechanics
drills.description: Hitter self-tosses and swings to a partner across the net or into the floor, focusing on high contact and wrist snap. Ten swings, switch.
drills.coachingPoints: Reach high, contact on top of the ball, snap your wrist over it.

drills.name: Hit Off Live Sets
drills.description: Passer-to-setter-to-hitter line: hitter passes a coach toss, transitions off the net, and attacks the setter's live set. Rotate through all three spots.
drills.coachingPoints: Get off the net after you pass — start your approach behind the ball, not under it.
skillTags: Hitting: Hitting off a live set

drills.name: Tip and Roll Placement Game
drills.description: Cones mark short, middle, and deep-corner zones. Hitters attack live sets but may only score with tips and roll shots into the called zone.
drills.coachingPoints: Same full approach every time — the tip only fools them if it looks like a swing.

drills.name: Net Shuffle Block Footwork
drills.description: Players shuffle along the net between three marked blocking spots, jumping with a full block at each one. Two lengths of the net, keeping hands up throughout.
drills.coachingPoints: Shuffle without crossing your feet and jump straight up — never drift into the net.

drills.name: Penetration Pin Blocks
drills.description: Partner stands on a box or across the net holding a ball above the tape; blocker jumps and presses both hands over the net onto the ball. Ten reps, switch.
drills.coachingPoints: Reach over, not up — press your hands into their side of the net.

drills.name: Read the Hitter
drills.description: Live hitter attacks off a set while one blocker watches approach angle and shoulders to time and position the block. Five attacks, rotate blocker and hitter.
drills.coachingPoints: Read their approach and shoulder — the hitter tells you where the ball is going.

drills.name: Block, Transition, Attack
drills.description: Blocker jumps against a coach attack at the net, lands, transitions off the net, and immediately attacks a set from the setter. Five full cycles each.
drills.coachingPoints: Land, turn, and sprint off the net — the block and the attack are one continuous play.

drills.name: Dig-Set-Hit Continuous
drills.description: Coach attacks at three defenders; they dig to the setter, set, and hit back over, then immediately defend the next attack. Play continuous two-minute rounds.
drills.coachingPoints: Low and stopped before the attack — dig the ball high and to the middle.

drills.name: 6v6 Wash Scrimmage
drills.description: Full-rotation scrimmage where a team must win two rallies in a row (serve rally plus a coach-entered free ball) to earn the point; a split washes to zero.
drills.coachingPoints: Talk every rally — call the ball, call the hitter, call the coverage.
